Provide Feedback

Lift Equation

The lift equation gives a mathematical model for lift in an educational aerodynamics setting. Lift depends on the lift coefficient, air density, velocity squared, and reference area. A common lesson form is L = Cl * A * .5 * r * V^2.

Equation Variables

The symbol Cl represents lift coefficient, A represents reference area, r represents density, and V represents velocity. The equation shows why lift changes when speed, area, air density, or coefficient values change. This page supports formula-aware ontology boundary analysis but does not provide aircraft-specific performance calculations for real flights.

Page Last Updated: May 14, 2024
